1-naive.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries for Sensorineural Hearing Loss

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|15188001,http://snomed.info/sct|72057004,http://snomed.info/sct|43943008&_include=Condition:subject
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.4,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.40,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.41,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.42,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.5,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.A1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.A2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.A3&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Code Coverage Breakdown

#### 🔵 SNOMED CT Codes
| Code | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `15188001` | Sensorineural hearing loss *(parent/unspecified)* |
| `72057004` | Bilateral sensorineural hearing loss |
| `43943008` | Unilateral sensorineural hearing loss |

#### 🟢 ICD-10-CM Codes
| Code | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `H90.3` | Sensorineural hearing loss, **bilateral** |
| `H90.4` | Sensorineural hearing loss, **unilateral** (parent) |
| `H90.40` | Unilateral — unspecified ear |
| `H90.41` | Unilateral — right ear |
| `H90.42` | Unilateral — left ear |
| `H90.5` | **Unspecified** sensorineural hearing loss |
| `H90.A1` | Unilateral, right ear, with restricted contralateral hearing |
| `H90.A2` | Unilateral, left ear, with restricted contralateral hearing |
| `H90.A3` | Unilateral, unspecified ear, with restricted contralateral hearing |

> **Note:** `_include=Condition:subject` returns the associated `Patient` resources in the same bundle. If your server supports SNOMED subsumption, you may alternatively use `Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|15188001` to catch all child concepts automatically.
1-naive.prompt_text: Find all patients who have been diagnosed with sensorineural hearing loss, including bilateral, unilateral, or unspecified types of sensorineural hearing impairment.

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries for Sensorineural Hearing Loss

### Using SNOMED CT (with subsumption to capture all subtypes)
```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|60700002&_include=Condition:subject
```

### Using ICD-10-CM Codes (Bilateral, Unilateral, and Unspecified)
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.41,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.42,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.5&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Code Reference

| Code System | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|
| SNOMED CT | `60700002` | Sensorineural hearing loss *(parent — `:below` captures all subtypes)* |
| ICD-10-CM | `H90.3` | Sensorineural hearing loss, **bilateral** |
| ICD-10-CM | `H90.41` | Sensorineural hearing loss, unilateral, **right ear** |
| ICD-10-CM | `H90.42` | Sensorineural hearing loss, unilateral, **left ear** |
| ICD-10-CM | `H90.5` | Sensorineural hearing loss, **unspecified** |

> **Notes:**
> - The SNOMED `:below` subsumption modifier is the most comprehensive approach, automatically covering bilateral (`44460000`), unilateral (`15188001`), and other child concepts without enumerating them explicitly.
> - `_include=Condition:subject` returns the associated **Patient** resources in the same bundle.
> - Run both queries if your system stores conditions using different coding systems, and de-duplicate by patient ID.
1-broad.prompt_text: Find all patients who have been diagnosed with sensorineural hearing loss, including bilateral, unilateral, or unspecified types of sensorineural hearing impairment.

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries: Sensorineural Hearing Loss

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|60700002&_include=Condition:subject

Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.41,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.42,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.5,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.A1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.A2&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Code Coverage Breakdown

| Code System | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|
| SNOMED CT | `60700002` | Sensorineural hearing loss *(parent concept — covers all subtypes if subsumption is enabled)* |
| ICD-10-CM | `H90.3` | Sensorineural hearing loss, **bilateral** |
| ICD-10-CM | `H90.41` | Sensorineural hearing loss, **unilateral, right ear** (unrestricted contralateral) |
| ICD-10-CM | `H90.42` | Sensorineural hearing loss, **unilateral, left ear** (unrestricted contralateral) |
| ICD-10-CM | `H90.5` | Sensorineural hearing loss, **unspecified** |
| ICD-10-CM | `H90.A1` | Sensorineural hearing loss, **unilateral, right ear** (restricted contralateral) |
| ICD-10-CM | `H90.A2` | Sensorineural hearing loss, **unilateral, left ear** (restricted contralateral) |

### Notes
- **`_include=Condition:subject`** returns matching `Patient` resources alongside the `Condition` records in a single Bundle.
- The **SNOMED query** relies on server-side subsumption support (`$subsumes`) to include child concepts — verify your FHIR server supports this.
- The **ICD-10-CM query** explicitly enumerates all bilateral, unilateral, and unspecified sensorineural codes (including ICD-10-CM 2021+ `H90.A*` codes) for servers without subsumption.
- Both queries should be run together for maximum coverage across coding systems.

1-naive.raw_response:
Here are the FHIR queries to find patients with hearing loss across multiple code systems and resource types:

**Conditions (SNOMED CT)**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|15188001&_include=Condition:patient
```

**Conditions (ICD-10-CM — Conductive & Sensorineural Hearing Loss)**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.0&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.1&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.2&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.3&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.4&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90.5&_include=Condition:patient
```

**Conditions (ICD-10-CM — Other/Unspecified Hearing Loss)**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H91.10&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H91.90&_include=Condition:patient
```

**Observations (Audiometry / Hearing Tests — LOINC)**
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|28615-3&_include=Observation:patient
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|89024-4&_include=Observation:patient
```

---

**Notes:**
- `H90.x` covers **conductive and sensorineural** hearing loss (bilateral, unilateral, mixed)
- `H91.x` covers **presbycusis** and unspecified hearing loss
- SNOMED `15188001` = *Hearing loss (disorder)* — broadest single-concept match
- LOINC `28615-3` = *Audiometry*, `89024-4` = *Hearing threshold* — useful for audiometric findings
- `_include=Condition:patient` / `_include=Observation:patient` retrieves associated Patient resources in the same bundle

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries — Hearing Loss (All Forms)

### 🔵 SNOMED CT — Subsumption (Preferred: catches all descendants)
```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|15188001&_include=Condition:patient
```

### 🔵 SNOMED CT — Explicit Key Subtypes (Fallback / Non-Subsumption Servers)
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|60700002,http://snomed.info/sct|194424002,http://snomed.info/sct|44057004,http://snomed.info/sct|49526009,http://snomed.info/sct|57972004,http://snomed.info/sct|15188001&_include=Condition:patient
```

### 🟠 ICD-10-CM — Conductive, Sensorineural & Mixed (H90.x)
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H90&_include=Condition:patient
```

### 🟠 ICD-10-CM — Presbycusis, Sudden & Other Hearing Loss (H91.x)
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H91&_include=Condition:patient
```

### 🟠 ICD-10-CM — Noise-Induced Inner Ear Damage (H83.3x)
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H83.3X1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H83.3X2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H83.3X3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|H83.3X9&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### 📋 Code Coverage Reference

| Category | SNOMED CT | ICD-10-CM |
|---|---|---|
| **Hearing loss (parent/broader)** | `15188001` | — |
| **Sensorineural** (bilateral/unilateral) | `60700002`, `194424002` | H90.3, H90.4x, H90.5 |
| **Conductive** (outer/middle ear) | `44057004` | H90.0, H90.1x, H90.2 |
| **Mixed** (conductive + sensorineural) | *(via :below)* | H90.6, H90.7x, H90.8 |
| **Presbycusis** (age-related) | `49526009` | H91.10–H91.13 |
| **Noise-induced** | `57972004` | H83.3X1–H83.3X9 |
| **Sudden idiopathic** | *(via :below)* | H91.20–H91.23 |

> **Note:** The `:below` (subsumption) modifier requires the FHIR server to have an active terminology service. The explicit-code queries serve as portable fallbacks for any system.
1-broad.prompt_text: Find patients diagnosed with any form of hearing loss — including sensorineural (most common, age-related/noise-induced), conductive (outer/middle ear cause), and the broader partial deafness concept.

1-expert.raw_response:
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|60700002,http://snomed.info/sct|44057004,http://snomed.info/sct|15188001
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| SNOMED Code | Concept | Clinical Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `60700002` | Sensorineural hearing loss | Cochlear/nerve-origin loss; most common subtype in adults |
| `44057004` | Conductive hearing loss | Mechanical/middle-ear origin; common in pediatric and otologic records |
| `15188001` | Hearing loss (Partial deafness) | Broader parent concept; captures cases coded at higher specificity levels |

---

### Design Notes

- **Single query, OR logic** — FHIR token parameters treat comma-separated values as a logical `OR`, so one round-trip retrieves all three phenotype arms simultaneously.
- **Diagnosis-only path** — Per your specification, no `MedicationRequest` or `Observation` legs are needed for this phenotype; all signal lives in `Condition`.
- **Recall strategy** — Including the parent concept `15188001` ensures you capture clinicians who document at the broader level without drilling into sensorineural vs. conductive, which is a common real-world coding pattern.
- **Extensibility tip** — If your system supports SNOMED subsumption (`code:below`), you can extend coverage with `Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|15188001` to automatically include all descendant concepts (e.g., mixed hearing loss, noise-induced), though explicit multi-code queries give more predictable behavior across FHIR servers that may not support `:below`.
